title: FOIA Request: Traffic Studies for La Grange Road

communications.subject: Illinois Freedom of Information Act. Request: FOIA Request: Traffic Studies for La Grange Road
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Illinois Freedom of Information Act., I hereby request the following records:

To the Freedom of Information Officer:

This is a request under the Illinois Freedom of Information Act (5 ILCS 140). I am requesting an electronic copy of the following public records:

The two (2) most recent traffic studies conducted by or on behalf of the Village of La Grange for La Grange Road (US-45/S. La Grange Rd) within the Village limits.

Request Details:

Format: I request these records be provided in an electronic format (PDF) and emailed.

Commercial Purpose: This request is not for a commercial purpose.

Fee Waiver: Since these records involve public safety and municipal planning, I request a waiver of any fees. If there are fees, please notify me before fulfilling the request.

Please respond within the five (5) business days required by the Act. If any portion of this request is denied, please cite the specific exemption and provide information on the appeals process.

Thank you for your assistance.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Concerned Citizen of La Grange

title: CMAP FOIA Request: South La Grange Road Corridor Plan

communications.subject: Illinois Freedom of Information Act. Request: CMAP FOIA Request: South La Grange Road Corridor Plan
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Illinois Freedom of Information Act., I hereby request the following records:

This is a request under the Illinois Freedom of Information Act (5 ILCS 140) for records related to the South La Grange Road Corridor Plan (Project Manager: Todd Vanadilok/Seth Thomas). I am requesting electronic copies of the following:

The full "South La Grange Road Corridor Existing Conditions Report" (and any technical appendices) finalized or presented circa July 2025.

The "Market Study" or "Economic/Real Estate Trends Analysis" associated with this corridor plan (completed circa late 2025/early 2026).

Any raw traffic count data, pedestrian/bicycle volume data, or safety/crash data collected or analyzed by CMAP or its consultants for La Grange Road between 47th Street and Plainfield Road for this project.

Any correspondence or reports regarding infrastructure capacity (specifically water, sewer, and stormwater) as it relates to potential redevelopment along this corridor.

Request Details:

Format: Please provide these records in electronic format (PDF/Excel) via email.

Commercial Purpose: This request is not for a commercial purpose.

Fee Waiver: This information is being requested for the purpose of community advocacy and public safety oversight. I request a waiver of all fees.

Please respond within five (5) business days. Thank you for your transparency and assistance in this planning process.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Concerned Citizen of La Grange

communications.subject: RE: Illinois Freedom of Information Act. Request: CMAP FOIA Request: South La Grange Road Corridor Plan
communications.communication:
Thank you for your email. We are working on your request.

Sincerely,

Blanca Vela-Schneider
FOIA Officer

communications.subject: RE: Illinois Freedom of Information Act. Request: CMAP FOIA Request: South La Grange Road Corridor Plan
communications.communication:
Good afternoon,

Attached is CMAP’s response to your FOIA request dated and received on Friday, March 13, 2026.

The project website includes the PDF links to the Existing Conditions Report and Market Study: https://engage.cmap.illinois.gov/s-la-grange-corridor-plan

Regarding the crash reports the file labeled crashes_la_grange_countryside.csv contains all the crashes within the municipal boundaries of LaGrange and Countryside. The others have filtered subsets that correspond to the counts on page 49 of the Existing Conditions Report. Our staff thought this metadata guide from the Federal Highway Administration’s (Highway Safety Information System Guidebook for the Illinois Data Files) would be helpful to you.  https://highways.dot.gov/sites/fhwa.dot.gov/files/FHWA-HRT-25-112.pdf

Respectfully,

Blanca Vela-Schneider
FOIA Officer

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: Treasury OIG - Closed Cases 2024
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

A copy of the list, printout, database listing of Inspector General investigations closed/reports completed during calendar year 2024. If the list is organized by FY rather than calendar year, the FY 2024 list is sufficient for this request. If no central list or database exists, then I request copies of the cover sheet or first page of each IG report completed during calendar year 2024.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Alan J. Segal

communications.subject: Your Request to Treasury 2026-FOIA-00873
communications.communication:
Dear Mr. Segal, 
This email concerns your Freedom of Information Act (FOIA)request to Treasury's Office of the Inspector General, dated 10/13/2025. 
ur apologies for the delays. Your request is currently inthe search phase of the process. Once all searches are completed, our officewill review the results and issue a response. FOIA requests are processed on a"First-In, First-Out" queue basis.  Please note that due to anextremely heavy FOIA backlog, we can only provide an estimated time ofcompletion. The estimated time of completion for your FOIA request is April 30,2026. 
This request has been issued tracking number 2026-FOIA-00873.Please reference this number in all future correspondence when communicatingabout this request. 

Sincerely, 

ReNay Mitchell 
FOIA & Transparency 
Office of Privacy, Transparency and Records

communications.subject: RE: Freedom of Information Act Request #2026-FOIA-00873
communications.communication:
Hi ReNay - thank you for the update. I can imagine there is quite a backlog at the moment. I appreciate you searching for records and look forward to your response. If you need any clarification from me please don't hesitate to reach out. 

All the best,
Alan

communications.subject: FOIA Auto-Reply
communications.communication:
[cid:image001.jpg@01D0A2E1.4C3000A0]

The U.S. Department of the Treasury has received the information you submitted to the FOIA@TREASURY.gov<mailto:FOIA@TREASURY.gov>  mailbox.

If you are submitting a FOIA request, this courtesy reply does not replace the acknowledgement letter which will include a FOIA case number once your request has been entered into our FOIA Request Tracking System.

If you are seeking the status of a pending FOIA request for which you have a FOIA case number, please call (202) 622-0930 to obtain the status of your request.

Please visit our FOIA Library at http://www.treasury.gov/FOIA/Pages/reading_room.aspx.

Thank you for contacting the U.S. Department of the Treasury.

title: Morgan County, WV Opioid Settlement Funds Spring 2026

communications.subject: West Virginia Freedom of Information Act Request: Morgan County, WV Opioid Settlement Funds Spring 2026
communications.communication:
Justin Rosa
1511 University Avenue, P.O. Box 6010
Morgantown, WV 26506-6010
Mar. 12 2026
Kimberly Johnson-Nickles
77 Fairfax St
Berkeley Springs, WV 25441
To whom ever it may concern, 
Under the West Virginia Freedom of Information Act, §29-B-1-1 et seq., I am requesting an opportunity to inspect or obtain copies of public records that pertain to Morgan County opioid settlement funds. Specifically, I am requesting: 
- Documentation of the amount of funds the county has received from the federal opioid lawsuit settlements from March 1, 2025 through today.
- The application forms that any organization would submit to receive settlement funds
- Documentation of how much county-level funding has been awarded from March 1, 2025 through today
- Any completed application of an organization that has received settlement funds from the county from March 1, 2025 through today. 
- Documentation of the process by which funds are awarded
- Any documents that contain the names of the individuals who review the funding applications and determine the awards
- Any documents that account for future plans from the county for the use of opioid settlement funds
If there are any fees for searching or copying these records, please inform me if the cost will exceed $25. However, I would also like to request a waiver of all fees in that the disclosure of the requested information is in the public interest and will contribute significantly to the public’s understanding of how the opioid settlement funds are being spent. I am a student journalist whose request is strictly for news-gathering purposes. This information is not being sought for commercial purposes. 
The West Virginia Freedom of Information Act requires a response to this request be made within five business days. If access to the records I am requesting will take longer than this amount of time, please contact me with information about when I might expect copies or the ability to inspect the requested records. 


If you deny any or all of this request, please cite each specific exemption you feel justifies the refusal to release the information and notify me of the appeal procedures available to me under the law. 
Thank you for considering my request. 
Sincerely, Justin Rosa
Justinrosabo3@gmail.com

title: DOJ Voter Data Request Communications (Secretary of State)

communications.subject: New Hampshire Right to Know Law Request: DOJ Voter Data Request Communications (Secretary of State)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the New Hampshire Right to Know Law, I hereby request the following records:

From January 20, 2025 to the present:

1. All correspondence, emails, letters, and written communications between the agency and the U.S. Department of Justice, including but not limited to the Civil Rights Division, the Office of the Attorney General, and the Executive Office for Immigration Review, relating to voter registration data, voter files, voter rolls, or voter list maintenance.

2. All internal communications within the agency (including emails, memoranda, and briefing materials) discussing, referencing, or responsive to any DOJ request for voter registration data or any proposed or executed memorandum of understanding with the DOJ regarding voter data.

3. Any lists, spreadsheets, databases, or other records received from the DOJ identifying specific voters for removal, investigation, or further review.

4. All records relating to the engagement of any third-party contractor in connection with DOJ voter data requests or voter list maintenance activities conducted pursuant to any agreement with the DOJ.

If any records are withheld in whole or in part, please identify the specific statutory exemption relied upon for each withholding.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Audrey Nielsen

communications.subject: RE: New Hampshire Right to Know Law Request: DOJ Voter Data Request Communications (Secretary of State)
communications.communication:
Good morning,

The New Hampshire Secretary of State’s Office has completed your Right to Know Request.

The production is provided via this secure download link<https://app.fed.everlaw.com/8050/dl/-TO_zSZUB0CIUft8jUO-rw6pcmfZpOhh5oGmXEYyVW-m>. After clicking the link, download the ZIP file and extract it to view the documents. The folder will contain a single PDF that has all of the responsive documents within it. It should be entitled “Documents.pdf.” You can ignore the text files.

You may wonder why there are so many news articles and/or public court filings that increased the number of documents in this production. These articles and court filings were shared internally and included discussion regarding the DOJ voter roll matter, and we felt it more transparent to include them in the disclosure. If you would prefer to exclude them, just let me know and I am happy to take them out and provide you with a new link.

This completes your Right to Know Request.

Have a nice weekend,



[Shape  Description automatically generated with low confidence]

communications.subject: Rhode Island Access to Public Records Act Request: Halal and Kosher Meals
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Rhode Island Access to Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

Ingredient lists and nutrition information for Halal and Kosher meals

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Sophia Wright

title: Procedures for Transportation of ICE Detainees

communications.subject: Rhode Island Access to Public Records Act Request: Procedures for Transportation of ICE Detainees
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Rhode Island Access to Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

Transportation procedures and contracts for the transportation of ICE detainees, including but not limited to: 
Policy for the timeline of informing ICE detainees in advance of transfer/deportation
Policy for personal possessions of ICE detainees during transfer
Security policies including use of restraints
Policies for bathroom facilities and food for ICE detainees during transfer 
Contracts pertaining to transportation of ICE detainees

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Sophia Wright

communications.subject: Rhode Island Access to Public Records Act Request: Emergency Contingency Plans
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Rhode Island Access to Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

Emergency contingency plans including:
Plans for assisting detainees with special needs
Chain of command
Emergency recall list
Facility access routes
Post emergency procedures
Fire control plan
Contracts with vendors for emergency evacuation

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Sophia Wright

title: Policies and procedures for inmates requesting a medical or mental health treatment

communications.subject: Rhode Island Access to Public Records Act Request: Policies and procedures for inmates requesting a medical or mental health treatment
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Rhode Island Access to Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

Written policies and procedures for inmates requesting a medical or mental health treatment (emergency and nonemergency). Written policies and procedures for ICE detainees requesting a medical or mental health treatment (emergency and nonemergency), if different.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Sophia Wright

communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Rhode Island Access to Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

Master-cycle menus; records of religious, special, therapeutic diets; and reviews of food service, including quarterly reviews and most recent review by a nutritionist or dietician between 2019 and 2026.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Sophia Wright

title: Minutes of the Executive Sessions of Board Meetings

communications.subject: Rhode Island Access to Public Records Act Request: Minutes of the Executive Sessions of Board Meetings
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Rhode Island Access to Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

Minutes of the Executive Sessions of the Central Falls Detention Facility Corporation Board Meetings between 2019 and 2026.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

communications.subject: Rhode Island Access to Public Records Act Request: Potable Water at the Wyatt Detention Facility
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

communications.subject: Rhode Island Access to Public Records Act Request: Procedures Pertaining to Inmate Medications
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Rhode Island Access to Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

Documentation of procedures pertaining to inmate medications between 2019 and 2026. Including:
- documentation of procedures pertaining to ICE detainee medications upon transfer to another facility
- inmate means of obtaining medication from family members
- inmate methods of obtaining prescriptions from outside providers (including acceptable methods of communication such as fax, mail or telephone)
- how medication is stored
- how medication is administered
- inmate methods of requesting non-prescription medications

Please provide responsive document to ANY of the above requests for records, even if records do not exist responsive to all of the requests.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Sophia Wright

communications.communication:

Pursuant to the Rhode Island Access to Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

Records of intra-system transfers in ICE custody from the Wyatt Detention Facility to facilities where ICE detainees are held, processed or transported between June 2025 and January 2026 inclusive. The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

title: NY police data collection request (Utica Police Department)

communications.subject: New York Freedom of Information Law Request: NY police data collection request (Utica Police Department)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the New York Freedom of Information Law, I hereby request the following records:

1. The total number of use of force incidents, separated by year used from 2016-2021 (for example, information that specifies the total number of use of force incidents in 2016, total number of use of force incidents in 2017, etc.).
2. The total number of use of force incidents, separated by type of force and year used from 2016-2021 (for example, information that specifies the number of taser incidents in 2016, baton incidents in 2017, etc.).
3. the total number of civilian complaints alleging any form of law enforcement misconduct that were reported and the total number sustained, separated by year from 2016-2021 (for example, 8 complaints in 2020, 2 complaints sustained in 2020, 4 complaints in 2021, 1 complaint sustained in 2021, etc.)
4. the total number of civilian complaints alleging law enforcement use of excessive force that were reported and the total number sustained from 2016-2021, separated by year
5. the total number of civilian complaints alleging biased policing or racial profiling that were reported and the total number sustained from 2016-2021, separated by year
6. the total number of officer involved shootings incidents from 2016-2021, separated by year (excluding shootings of animals and accidental discharges that are not directed at a person)

When available, I would appreciate being sent individualized data for each item in this request in spreadsheet format. If individualized data are unavailable, please send information that includes aggregate statistics for the item instead.

Note that my requests for civilian complaints are specifically for civilian complaints alleging police misconduct, please do not include complaints filed by one officer/the department against another officer.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Samuel Sinyangwe

communications.subject: RE: Freedom of Information Act Request: 321D-IR-A7788
communications.communication:
Please check the status of your FOIPA Request at http://vault.fbi.gov by clicking on "Check Status of Your FOI/PA Request" on the right side of the page, and follow the instructions below.

Check the Status of Your FOIPA Request
If your FOIPA Number is [1234567-0] please enter [1234567-000] into the system. If your FOIPA Number is [1234567-1] please enter [1234567-001] into the system. If you have any questions about the status of your FOIPA request, please e-mail foipaquestions@ic.fbi.gov.

FIND STATUS OF FOIPA- Request statuses are updated weekly

Please enter the whole FOIPA number-Example: [1234567-000]

FOIPA:
Results will show the Request Number, Case Type and Process Description shown below:
FOIPA:
1234567-000

Case Type:
FOIPA

Process Description (Will display the current progress of the request)
The FBI's FOIPA Program is searching the FBI's indices for potentially responsive documents.
You may be contacted via formal letter for all fees and/or negotiation issues that may apply.

NOTE: Recent requests are entered into the FOIPA database in the order that they are received. Before you can check the status, you must have received correspondence assigning a FOIPA request number and the information transferred to the online database. Status information is updated weekly. If a request has been closed within the last six months the online database will display the following: The FOIPA number entered has been closed, and appropriate correspondence has been sent to the address on file.

Estimated Dates of Completion
Requests are processed in the order in which they are received through our multi-track processing system.  Requests are divided into two primary tracks--simple (under 50 pages of potentially responsive documents) and complex (over 50 pages of potentially responsive documents).  Complex requests are further divided into medium, large, and extra-large sub-tracks based upon request size.  Simple track requests typically require the least amount of time to process.  Currently, simple track cases average approximately 134 days from the date of receipt for processing.  Our complex requests in the medium processing track are currently averaging 523 days, large processing track are currently averaging approximately 1,335 days, and extra-large processing track are currently averaging 1,875 days for processing.

Respectfully,

Public Information Officer
Record/Information Dissemination Section (RIDS) FBI-Information Management Division
170 Marcel Drive, Winchester, VA 22602-4843
Direct: (540) 868-4593
Fax: (540) 868-4391
Questions E-mail: foipaquestions@fbi.gov<mailto:foipaquestions@fbi.gov>

Do you have further questions about the FOI/PA process? Visit us at http://www.fbi.gov/foia

Please check the status of your request online at https://vault.fbi.gov/fdps-1/@@search-fdps    Status updates are performed on a weekly basis.

Note:  This is a non-emergency email address.  If this is an emergency, please call 911 directly.  If you need to report a tip for immediate action, please contact FBI Tips at http://tips.fbi.gov/ or reach out to your local field office.

title: Sheriff use of force data (2017-2025) (Johnson County Sheriff)

communications.subject: Texas Public Information Act Request: Sheriff use of force data (2017-2025) (Johnson County Sheriff)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Texas Public Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

Use of force (UOF) data dating from January 1, 2017 to December 31, 2025. If possible, please provide this data in database or spreadsheet format and include the case number, incident date, incident time, subject injured indicator, subject injury force related info (e.g. Yes or No), subject age, subject gender, subject ethnicity, subject weapon type, incident area occurred, force type, officer division (e.g. law enforcement operations, detention/correctional operations, etc.), badge number and names associated with the officers involved. If one or more of these fields or dates is unavailable, provide all fields and/or information that is available responsive to my request. If no database or spreadsheet format is available, please provide copies of each individual use of force report or other report where use of force information is documented/reported by officers. Note, this is for all use of force incidents reported by your agency, not limited to only those incidents required to be reported under state or federal programs.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Samuel Sinyangwe

communications.subject: RE: Texas Public Information Act Request: Sheriff use of force data (2017-2025) (Johnson County Sheriff)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

I wanted to follow up on the following Texas Public Information Act request, copied below, and originally submitted on Jan. 30, 2026. Please let me know when I can expect to receive a response.

Thanks for your help, and let me know if further clarification is needed.

communications.subject: RE: Texas Public Information Act Request: Sheriff use of force data (2017-2025) (Johnson County Sheriff)
communications.communication:
Dear Mr. Sinyangwe:

In response to your request to the Johnson County Sheriff’s Office dated January 30, 2026 the Sheriff's Office and the IT Department have been working to assimilate the requested data.   Due to a recent change in Record Management System the Sheriff’s Office and IT Department has been performing programming tasks to facilitate access to the some of the records you requested. We believe that process is near completion. Once the Sheriff’s Office has “pulled” the requested documents they will be reviewed to determine if they contain information which must be redacted.  Following review and any necessary redaction the material will be sent to you.  Because you requested several years of information, the material may be sent in “tranches” for each year, or some other logical division. I anticipate you will begin receiving data on or about March 2, 2026 and continuing over the following weeks as volume and workload allows.

Respectfully,

Shauna Ballard
Open Records Clerk III
Johnson County Sheriff’s Office
1102 E. Kilpatrick Suite A.
Cleburne, TX 76031
817-556-6058 ext. 3561
sballard@johnsoncountytx.org<mailto:sballard@johnsoncountytx.org>
[cid:image001.jpg@01DC9D0B.C1B5A3E0]

Shauna Ballard

communications.subject: California Public Records Act Request: Fairfax RIPA requests
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the California Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

1) Any and all records reflecting data collected and recorded by the Police Department in accordance with the California Racial and Identity Profiling Act (RIPA) from January 1, 2025, through the present. Please provide the raw data in excel or csv format.

2) Any and all reports, records, and analyses created by, for, or at the direction of, the City/Town that pertain to any RIPA data that has been collected by the Police Department since January 1, 2025.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 calendar days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Tammy Edmonson

communications.subject: RE: California Public Records Act Request: Fairfax RIPA requests
communications.communication:
Good evening Ms. Edmonson,

Thank you for the request.  Our RIPA data is hosted on our department website.  You can access our RIPA data, including the parameters of your request, using the following link: Racial and Identify Profiling Act (RIPA) - Town of Fairfax<https://townoffairfaxca.gov/racial-and-identify-profiling-act-ripa/>.  From the link, you can access our RIPA Dashboard which is periodically updated.

Respectfully,

Lieutenant William Cade #093
Fairfax Police Department
144 Bolinas Rd, Fairfax, CA. 94930<x-apple-data-detectors://6/1>

communications.subject: Pennsylvania Right to Know Act Request: FOIA logs (01/01/2024 - present) (Mayor's Office)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Pennsylvania Right to Know Act, I hereby request the following records:

I request a complete copy of any logs, spreadsheets, or databases maintained by this agency that track public records requests received from January 1, 2024 until this request is fulfilled (inclusive of those dates).

Please include all available information, including (if available):

• Date the request was filed
• Requester name
• Organizational affiliation of the requester
• Summary or description of request
• Request response date
• Status of the request
• Fee charged, if any
• Request System assigned identifier such as a Request Number, if applicable
• Whether any materials were released as a result of the request

Please provide the data in spreadsheet or machine-readable format (e.g., CSV or Excel).

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Silvia Canelón

files.title: PA Right to Know Request  Form
files.source: MuckRock

communications.subject: Mayor's Office - FOIA logs (01/01/2024 - present) (Mayor's Office)
communications.communication:
THE CITY OF PHILADELPHIA      Re: Mayor's Office - FOIA logs (01/01/2024 - present) (Mayor's Office)    Your receipt number is: 35609  Dear 199616-62646222@requests. muckrock. com, 

   

 Thank you for your correspondence.  This is an automatic email confirming that your correspondence has been entered into our case management system for processing and response. If you have an additional request you wish to file, or if you wish to make a change to a pending request, please email it directly to righttoknowlaw@.phila.gov.     Did you know that the City already releases a lot of information and open data online?  You might be able to find the answer to your inquiry right now!   

   

 

Here are some common types of requests we receive and where to find this information online right now:  

 

 Search for a property's L&I related permits, licenses, violations, and appeals with the L&I property history tool.  

 

 Search for a property's Real Estate Tax Balance with the Revenue Department's tax real estate lookup tool.  

 

 Find all of the City of Philadelphia's open data sets, including City employee salary data, on Open Data Philly, the Philadelphia region's open data portal.  

 

 Search information about professional service contracts online at eContract Philly.  

 

 Search information about procurement contracts online with PHL Contracts.  

 

 Search City legislation and related hearings online with Legistar.  

 

 Obtain financial disclosure forms from the Department of Records.  

 

 

   If you seek a police incident report, you can obtain the proper form from the Department of Records.  Please note that police incident reports cost $25.      If you seek audio, visual, or bodycam records from the Philadelphia Police Department, you will need to make a request following the instructions in 42 Pa.C.S.A. Sec. 67A03 at https://www.legis.state.pa.us/cfdocs/legis/LI/consCheck.cfm?txtType=HTM&ttl=42&div=0&chpt=67A. Such requests must be made in-person or via 

certified mail following the instructions in 42 Pa.C.S.A. §  67A(3) (aka

 Act 22). Such requests are not processed under the RTKL. See 42 Pa.C.S.A. § 67A(2)(a); 65 P.S. §§ 67.305, 67.3101.1. You may use this form to make a request under Act 22. Requests under Act 22  should be addressed to: Lieutenant Barry Jacobs, Philadelphia Police Department, Open Records/Right to Know Section, Philadelphia Public Services Building, 400 N. Broad St., 4W-72, Philadelphia, PA 19130. Such requests are not processed under the Right-to-Know Law.       Use the forms on this page to request copies of various public safety from the Department of Records, such as:   

 Traffic Accident Reports (aka "crash report" - also available through https://public-safety-reports.phila.gov/)  A records check or letter of good conduct from the Philadelphia Police Department  Fire Reports  Emergency Medical Services Reports       The City of Philadelphia does not process Right-to-Know requests for the organizations listed below.  If you want to submit a request to one of these entities, please click the links below to be directed to their Open Records Policies:   

 

Philadelphia Parking Authority  

 

 

School District of Philadelphia  

 

 

City of Philadelphia District Attorney’s Office  

 

 

Philadelphia Redevelopment Authority  

 

 

First Judicial District (Philadelphia Court of Common Pleas)  

 

 

   If one of these sources has solved your inquiry, please let us know!  Otherwise, thank you for your patience, and we will be in touch soon.   Sincerely,   The City of Philadelphia  Please Note: To help make sure that future emails from us don't end up in your spam or junk folder, please add this email address to your safe sender list.

communications.subject: Re: Mayor's Office - FOIA logs (01/01/2024 - present) (Mayor's Office)
communications.communication:
CITY OF PHILADELPHIA  LAW DEPARTMENT One Parkway Building 1515 Arch Street Philadelphia, PA 19102  Renee Garcia  City Solicitor   BY EMAIL: 199616-62646222@requests.muckrock.com Re: Mayor's Office - FOIA logs (01/01/2024 - present) (Mayor's Office) Request #: 35609  Dear 199616-62646222@requests. muckrock. com:  Thank you for writing to the City of Philadelphia (City) with your request for information. This correspondence serves as an acknowledgement and receipt of your request as well as notice of the City’s Open Records Policy pursuant to the Right-to-Know Law.  Written requests for records to state and local agencies in Pennsylvania are generally governed by the Commonwealth’s Right-to-Know Law (RTKL), 65 P.S. §§ 67.101-67.3104. In accordance with the RTKL, the City has promulgated an Open Records Policy, which requires the standard statewide Right-to-Know Form be used for the submission of written requests pursuant to the RTKL. See 65 P.S. § 67.504(a); Dekok v. Lancaster County, AP 2010-1197, 2011 PA O.O.R.D. LEXIS 25 at *5 and n.1 (Pa. O.O.R. Jan. 17, 2011) (holding that in accordance with the RTKL, an agency may adopt a policy requiring the use of the standard statewide form established by the Office of Open Records in order to file a RTK Request); accord Pa. Gaming Control Bd. v. Office of Open Records, 48 A.3d 503 (Pa. Cmmw. 2012). As your request was not submitted on or with the standard statewide form, the City does not consider your request to be a Right-to-Know Request pursuant to the RTKL and will process it as an informal request.  You do not have to resubmit your request for the City to respond unless you are specifically informed otherwise. The City reserves the right to require a formal RTK Request be submitted pursuant to the RTKL before releasing records. The City is continuing to process the request you have submitted as an informal request outside the RTKL and will be in touch with you further to respond to your request.  However, if you would like to file a RTK Request, please submit your request on or with the standard statewide Right-to-Know Form and email your request to righttoknowlaw@phila.gov – please do not reply to this email. Please include this notice with your RTK Request. Please note that if you resubmit your request as a written request pursuant to the RTKL, the City will deem your informal request to have been withdrawn.   The City’s Open Records Policy can be found here:   https://www.phila.gov/open-records-policy/   The statewide form appears at the end of the City’s Open Records Policy. If you require a hard copy of the form and/or policy, please let me know and one will be provided.  The City reserves the right to assert any and all grounds of denial in its final response to your request.  Respectfully,  Margot B. Smith    Chief Deputy City Solicitor    Right to Know Unit  City of Philadelphia Law Department    Tracking #: Com 35609 Request Summary: Pursuant to the Pennsylvania Right to Know Act, I hereby request the following records: I request a complete copy of any logs, spreadsheets, or databases maintained by this agency that track public records requests received from January 1, 2024 until this request is fulfilled (inclusive of those dates). Please include all available information, including (if available): • Date the request was filed • Requester name • Organizational affiliation of the requester • Summary or description of request • Request response date • Status of the request • Fee charged, if any • Request System assigned identifier such as a Request Number, if applicable • Whether any materials were released as a result of the request Original Request:   

   

 

 

 

From:

199616-62646222@requests.muckrock.com <199616-62646222@requests.muckrock.com>

Sec. 67A03 at https://www.legis.state.pa.us/cfdocs/legis/LI/consCheck.cfm?txtType=HTM&ttl=42&div=0&chpt=67A. Such requests must be made in-person or via 

certified mail following the instructions in 42 Pa.C.S.A. §  67A(3) (aka

 Act 22). Such requests are not processed under the RTKL. See 42 Pa.C.S.A. § 67A(2)(a); 65 P.S. §§ 67.305, 67.3101.1. You may use this form to make a request under Act 22. Requests under Act 22  should be addressed to: Lieutenant Barry Jacobs, Philadelphia Police Department, Open Records/Right to Know Section, Philadelphia Public Services Building, 400 N. Broad St., 4W-72, Philadelphia, PA 19130. Such requests are not processed under the Right-to-Know Law.       Use the forms on this page to request copies of various public safety from the Department of Records, such as:   

 Traffic Accident Reports (aka "crash report" - also available through https://public-safety-reports.phila.gov/)  A records check or letter of good conduct from the Philadelphia Police Department  Fire Reports  Emergency Medical Services Reports       The City of Philadelphia does not process Right-to-Know requests for the organizations listed below.  If you want to submit a request to one of these entities, please click the links below to be directed to their Open Records Policies:   

 

Philadelphia Parking Authority  

 

 

School District of Philadelphia  

 

 

City of Philadelphia District Attorney’s Office  

 

 

Philadelphia Redevelopment Authority  

 

 

First Judicial District (Philadelphia Court of Common Pleas)  

 

 

   If one of these sources has solved your inquiry, please let us know!  Otherwise, thank you for your patience, and we will be in touch soon.   Sincerely,   The City of Philadelphia  Please Note: To help make sure that future emails from us don't end up in your spam or junk folder, please add this email address to your safe sender list.

communications.subject: Re: Mayor's Office - complete copy of public record request log for the Mayor's Office from 1/1/24 to date
communications.communication: CITY OF PHILADELPHIA  LAW DEPARTMENT One Parkway Building 1515 Arch Street Philadelphia, PA 19102  Renee Garcia  City Solicitor   BY EMAIL: 199616-62646222@requests.muckrock.com Re: Mayor's Office - complete copy of public record request log for the Mayor's Office from 1/1/24 to date Request #: 36600  Dear 199616-62646222@requests. muckrock. com:  Thank you for writing to the City of Philadelphia (City) with your request for information. This correspondence serves as an acknowledgement and receipt of your request as well as notice of the City’s Open Records Policy pursuant to the Right-to-Know Law.  Written requests for records to state and local agencies in Pennsylvania are generally governed by the Commonwealth’s Right-to-Know Law (RTKL), 65 P.S. §§ 67.101-67.3104. In accordance with the RTKL, the City has promulgated an Open Records Policy, which requires the standard statewide Right-to-Know Form be used for the submission of written requests pursuant to the RTKL. See 65 P.S. § 67.504(a); Dekok v. Lancaster County, AP 2010-1197, 2011 PA O.O.R.D. LEXIS 25 at *5 and n.1 (Pa. O.O.R. Jan. 17, 2011) (holding that in accordance with the RTKL, an agency may adopt a policy requiring the use of the standard statewide form established by the Office of Open Records in order to file a RTK Request); accord Pa. Gaming Control Bd. v. Office of Open Records, 48 A.3d 503 (Pa. Cmmw. 2012). As your request was not submitted on or with the standard statewide form, the City does not consider your request to be a Right-to-Know Request pursuant to the RTKL and will process it as an informal request.  You do not have to resubmit your request for the City to respond unless you are specifically informed otherwise. The City reserves the right to require a formal RTK Request be submitted pursuant to the RTKL before releasing records. The City is continuing to process the request you have submitted as an informal request outside the RTKL and will be in touch with you further to respond to your request.  However, if you would like to file a RTK Request, please submit your request on or with the standard statewide Right-to-Know Form and email your request to righttoknowlaw@phila.gov – please do not reply to this email. Please include this notice with your RTK Request. Please note that if you resubmit your request as a written request pursuant to the RTKL, the City will deem your informal request to have been withdrawn.   The City’s Open Records Policy can be found here:   https://www.phila.gov/open-records-policy/   The statewide form appears at the end of the City’s Open Records Policy. If you require a hard copy of the form and/or policy, please let me know and one will be provided.  The City reserves the right to assert any and all grounds of denial in its final response to your request.  Respectfully,  Margot B. This correspondence constitutes the final response to your request by the City Law Department on behalf of its client, the City of Philadelphia. See Philadelphia Home Rule Charter, § 4-400.       Your request is granted. Responsive records are attached.       This correspondence will close your request with our office as permitted by law.   Respectfully,  Margot B. Smith     --  Margot B. Smith (she/her)    Chief Deputy City Solicitor – Right to Know  Compliance & Legislation Group  City of Philadelphia Law Department    1515 Arch Street, 17th floor     Philadelphia, PA 19102    Margot.Smith@phila.gov    RTKL Links: https://linktr.ee/phillylaw        ​*As explained in correspondence sent to you on Feb 27, 2026​, written requests for records to state and local agencies in Pennsylvania are generally governed by the Commonwealth’s Right to Know Law (RTKL), 65 P.S. § 67.101 et seq. In accordance with RTKL, the City has promulgated an Open Records Policy, which requires use of the standard statewide Right to Know Form for the submission of written requests pursuant to RTKL (“RTK Requests”). See 65 P.S. § 67.504(a); Dekok v. Lancaster County, AP 2010-1197, 2011 PA O.O.R.D. LEXIS 25 at *5 and n.1 (Pa. O.O.R. Jan. 17, 2011) (holding that in accordance with RTKL, an agency may adopt a policy requiring the use of the standard statewide form established by the Office of Open Records in order to file an RTK Request); accord Pa. Gaming Control Bd. v. Office of Open Records, 48 A.3d 503 (Pa. Commw. 2012). This policy is available online at https://www.phila.gov/open-records-policy/ To the extent you intended to submit an RTK Request, your request is denied as you have not complied with the City’s Open Records Policy. As your request was not submitted on or with the standard statewide form, the City does not consider your request to be a RTK Request pursuant to RTKL and has processed it as an informal request. The City nonetheless referred to RTKL for guidance in determining whether records should be released in response to your request.

communications.subject: Pennsylvania Right to Know Act Request: FOIA logs (01/01/2024 - present) (City Of Philadelphia)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Pennsylvania Right to Know Act, I hereby request the following records:

I request a complete copy of any logs, spreadsheets, or databases maintained by this agency that track public records requests received from January 1, 2024 until this request is fulfilled (inclusive of those dates).

Please include all available information, including (if available):

• Date the request was filed
• Requester name
• Organizational affiliation of the requester
• Summary or description of request
• Request response date
• Status of the request
• Fee charged, if any
• Request System assigned identifier such as a Request Number, if applicable
• Whether any materials were released as a result of the request

Please provide the data in spreadsheet or machine-readable format (e.g., CSV or Excel).

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Silvia Canelón

communications.subject: Law - FOIA logs (01/01/2024 - present) (City Of Philadelphia)
communications.communication:
THE CITY OF PHILADELPHIA      Re: Law - FOIA logs (01/01/2024 - present) (City Of Philadelphia)    Your receipt number is: 35597  Dear 199615-39590937@requests. muckrock. com, 

   

 Thank you for your correspondence.  This is an automatic email confirming that your correspondence has been entered into our case management system for processing and response. If you have an additional request you wish to file, or if you wish to make a change to a pending request, please email it directly to righttoknowlaw@.phila.gov.     Did you know that the City already releases a lot of information and open data online?  You might be able to find the answer to your inquiry right now!   

   

 

Here are some common types of requests we receive and where to find this information online right now:  

 

 Search for a property's L&I related permits, licenses, violations, and appeals with the L&I property history tool.  

 

 Search for a property's Real Estate Tax Balance with the Revenue Department's tax real estate lookup tool.  

 

 Find all of the City of Philadelphia's open data sets, including City employee salary data, on Open Data Philly, the Philadelphia region's open data portal.  

 

 Search information about professional service contracts online at eContract Philly.  

 

 Search information about procurement contracts online with PHL Contracts.  

 

 Search City legislation and related hearings online with Legistar.  

 

 Obtain financial disclosure forms from the Department of Records.  

 

 

   If you seek a police incident report, you can obtain the proper form from the Department of Records.  Please note that police incident reports cost $25.      If you seek audio, visual, or bodycam records from the Philadelphia Police Department, you will need to make a request following the instructions in 42 Pa.C.S.A. Sec. 67A03 at https://www.legis.state.pa.us/cfdocs/legis/LI/consCheck.cfm?txtType=HTM&ttl=42&div=0&chpt=67A. Such requests must be made in-person or via 

certified mail following the instructions in 42 Pa.C.S.A. §  67A(3) (aka

 Act 22). Such requests are not processed under the RTKL. See 42 Pa.C.S.A. § 67A(2)(a); 65 P.S. §§ 67.305, 67.3101.1. You may use this form to make a request under Act 22. Requests under Act 22  should be addressed to: Lieutenant Barry Jacobs, Philadelphia Police Department, Open Records/Right to Know Section, Philadelphia Public Services Building, 400 N. Broad St., 4W-72, Philadelphia, PA 19130. Such requests are not processed under the Right-to-Know Law.       Use the forms on this page to request copies of various public safety from the Department of Records, such as:   

 Traffic Accident Reports (aka "crash report" - also available through https://public-safety-reports.phila.gov/)  A records check or letter of good conduct from the Philadelphia Police Department  Fire Reports  Emergency Medical Services Reports       The City of Philadelphia does not process Right-to-Know requests for the organizations listed below.  If you want to submit a request to one of these entities, please click the links below to be directed to their Open Records Policies:   

 

Philadelphia Parking Authority  

 

 

School District of Philadelphia  

 

 

City of Philadelphia District Attorney’s Office  

 

 

Philadelphia Redevelopment Authority  

 

 

First Judicial District (Philadelphia Court of Common Pleas)  

 

 

   If one of these sources has solved your inquiry, please let us know!  Otherwise, thank you for your patience, and we will be in touch soon.   Sincerely,   The City of Philadelphia  Please Note: To help make sure that future emails from us don't end up in your spam or junk folder, please add this email address to your safe sender list.

communications.subject: Re: Law - FOIA logs (01/01/2024 - present) (City Of Philadelphia)
communications.communication: CITY OF PHILADELPHIA  LAW DEPARTMENT One Parkway Building 1515 Arch Street Philadelphia, PA 19102  Renee Garcia  City Solicitor   BY EMAIL: 199615-39590937@requests.muckrock.com Re: Law - FOIA logs (01/01/2024 - present) (City Of Philadelphia) Request #: 35597  Dear 199615-39590937@requests. muckrock. com:  Thank you for writing to the City of Philadelphia (City) with your request for information. This correspondence serves as an acknowledgement and receipt of your request as well as notice of the City’s Open Records Policy pursuant to the Right-to-Know Law.  Written requests for records to state and local agencies in Pennsylvania are generally governed by the Commonwealth’s Right-to-Know Law (RTKL), 65 P.S. §§ 67.101-67.3104. In accordance with the RTKL, the City has promulgated an Open Records Policy, which requires the standard statewide Right-to-Know Form be used for the submission of written requests pursuant to the RTKL. See 65 P.S. § 67.504(a); Dekok v. Lancaster County, AP 2010-1197, 2011 PA O.O.R.D. LEXIS 25 at *5 and n.1 (Pa. O.O.R. Jan. 17, 2011) (holding that in accordance with the RTKL, an agency may adopt a policy requiring the use of the standard statewide form established by the Office of Open Records in order to file a RTK Request); accord Pa. Gaming Control Bd. v. Office of Open Records, 48 A.3d 503 (Pa. Cmmw. 2012). As your request was not submitted on or with the standard statewide form, the City does not consider your request to be a Right-to-Know Request pursuant to the RTKL and will process it as an informal request.  You do not have to resubmit your request for the City to respond unless you are specifically informed otherwise. The City reserves the right to require a formal RTK Request be submitted pursuant to the RTKL before releasing records. The City is continuing to process the request you have submitted as an informal request outside the RTKL and will be in touch with you further to respond to your request.  However, if you would like to file a RTK Request, please submit your request on or with the standard statewide Right-to-Know Form and email your request to righttoknowlaw@phila.gov – please do not reply to this email. Please include this notice with your RTK Request. Please note that if you resubmit your request as a written request pursuant to the RTKL, the City will deem your informal request to have been withdrawn.   The City’s Open Records Policy can be found here:   https://www.phila.gov/open-records-policy/   The statewide form appears at the end of the City’s Open Records Policy. If you require a hard copy of the form and/or policy, please let me know and one will be provided.  The City reserves the right to assert any and all grounds of denial in its final response to your request.  Respectfully,  Margot B. Smith    Chief Deputy City Solicitor    Right to Know Unit  City of Philadelphia Law Department    Tracking #: Com 35597 Request Summary: Pursuant to the Pennsylvania Right to Know Act, I hereby request the following records: I request a complete copy of any logs, spreadsheets, or databases maintained by this agency that track public records requests received from January 1, 2024 until this request is fulfilled (inclusive of those dates). Please include all available information, including (if available): • Date the request was filed • Requester name • Organizational affiliation of the requester • Summary or description of request • Request response date • Status of the request • Fee charged, if any • Request System assigned identifier such as a Request Number, if applicable • Whether any materials were released as a result of the request Original Request:

certified mail following the instructions in 42 Pa.C.S.A. §  67A(3) (aka

communications.communication:
Re: Law - copy of all public record request tracking data from 1/1/24 to date Request #: 36599  Dear 199615-39590937@requests. muckrock. com:  Thank you for writing to the City of Philadelphia (City) with your inquiry* received on Feb 20, 2026. This correspondence constitutes the final response to your request by the City Law Department on behalf of its client, the City of Philadelphia. See Philadelphia Home Rule Charter, § 4-400.       Your request is granted. Responsive records are attached.       This correspondence will close your request with our office as permitted by law.   Respectfully,  Margot B. Smith     --  Margot B. Smith (she/her)    Chief Deputy City Solicitor – Right to Know  Compliance & Legislation Group  City of Philadelphia Law Department    1515 Arch Street, 17th floor     Philadelphia, PA 19102    Margot.Smith@phila.gov    RTKL Links: https://linktr.ee/phillylaw        *As explained in correspondence sent to you on Feb 27, 2026, written requests for records to state and local agencies in Pennsylvania are generally governed by the Commonwealth’s Right to Know Law (RTKL), 65 P.S. § 67.101 et seq. In accordance with RTKL, the City has promulgated an Open Records Policy, which requires use of the standard statewide Right to Know Form for the submission of written requests pursuant to RTKL (“RTK Requests”). See 65 P.S. § 67.504(a); Dekok v. Lancaster County, AP 2010-1197, 2011 PA O.O.R.D. LEXIS 25 at *5 and n.1 (Pa. O.O.R. Jan. 17, 2011) (holding that in accordance with RTKL, an agency may adopt a policy requiring the use of the standard statewide form established by the Office of Open Records in order to file an RTK Request); accord Pa. Gaming Control Bd. v. Office of Open Records, 48 A.3d 503 (Pa. Commw. 2012). This policy is available online at https://www.phila.gov/open-records-policy/ To the extent you intended to submit an RTK Request, your request is denied as you have not complied with the City’s Open Records Policy. As your request was not submitted on or with the standard statewide form, the City does not consider your request to be a RTK Request pursuant to RTKL and has processed it as an informal request. The City nonetheless referred to RTKL for guidance in determining whether records should be released in response to your request.              

                           

                            On

                            Fri, Feb 27 at  9:45 AM

                            ,  City of Philadelphia Right to Know <righttoknow@phila.gov>  wrote:

                           

                           

                             CITY OF PHILADELPHIA     LAW DEPARTMENT   One Parkway Building   1515 Arch Street   Philadelphia, PA 19102     ​Renee Garcia   City Solicitor     BY EMAIL: 199615-39590937@requests.muckrock.com   Re: Law - copy of all public record request tracking data from 1/1/24 to date   Request #: 36599     Dear 199615-39590937@requests. muckrock. com:     Thank you for writing to the City of Philadelphia (City) with your request for information. This correspondence serves as an acknowledgement and receipt of your request as well as notice of the City’s Open Records Policy pursuant to the Right-to-Know Law.     Written requests for records to state and local agencies in Pennsylvania are generally governed by the Commonwealth’s Right to Know Law (RTKL), 65 P.S. §§ 67.101-67.3104. In accordance with the RTKL, the City has promulgated an Open Records Policy, which requires the standard statewide Right-to-Know Form be used for the submission of written requests pursuant to the RTKL. See 65 P.S. § 67.504(a); Dekok v. Lancaster County, AP 2010-1197, 2011 PA O.O.R.D. LEXIS 25 at *5 and n.1 (Pa. O.O.R. Jan. 17, 2011) (holding that in accordance with the RTKL, an agency may adopt a policy requiring the use of the standard statewide form established by the Office of Open Records in order to file a RTK Request); accord Pa. Gaming Control Bd. v. Office of Open Records, 48 A.3d 503 (Pa. Cmmw. 2012). As your request was not submitted on or with the standard statewide form, the City does not consider your request to be a RTK Request pursuant to RTKL and will process it as an informal request.     You do not have to resubmit your request for the City to respond unless you are specifically informed otherwise. The City reserves the right to require a formal Right-to-Know Request be submitted pursuant to the RTKL before releasing records. The City is continuing to process the request you have submitted as an informal request outside the RTKL and will be in touch with you further to respond to your request.     However, if you would like to file a RTK Request, please submit your request on or with the standard statewide Right-to-Know Form and email your request to righttoknowlaw@phila.gov - please do not reply to this email. Please include this notice with your RTK Request. Please note that if you resubmit your request as a written request pursuant to the RTKL, the City will deem your informal request to have been withdrawn.     The City’s Open Records Policy can be found here: https://www.phila.gov/open-records-policy/     The statewide form appears at the end of the City’s Open Records Policy. If you require a hard copy of the form and/or policy, please let me know and one will be provided.     The City reserves the right to assert any and all grounds of denial in its final response to your request.     Respectfully,     Margot B. Smith  Chief Deputy City Solicitor, Right to Know Unit, City of Philadelphia Law Department  (215) 683-5069 (fax)      Tracking #: Com 36599   Request Summary: Pursuant to the Pennsylvania Right to Know Act, I hereby request the following records:  I request a complete copy of any logs, spreadsheets, or databases maintained by this agency that track public records requests received from January 1, 2024 until this request is fulfilled (inclusive of those dates).  Please include all available information, including (if available):  • Date the request was filed  • Requester name  • Organizational affiliation of the requester  • Summary or description of request  • Request response date  • Status of the request  • Fee charged, if any  • Request System assigned identifier such as a Request Number, if applicable  • Whether any materials were released as a result of the request   Original Request:  

 

 

 Pursuant to the Pennsylvania Right to Know Act, I hereby request the following records: 

 I request a complete copy of any logs, spreadsheets, or databases maintained by this agency that track public records requests received from January 1, 2024 until this request is fulfilled (inclusive of those dates). 

 Please include all available information, including (if available): 

 • Date the request was filed  • Requester name  • Organizational affiliation of the requester  • Summary or description of request  • Request response date  • Status of the request  • Fee charged, if any  • Request System assigned identifier such as a Request Number, if applicable  • Whether any materials were released as a result of the request 

   

 February 20, 2026 

 This is a follow up to request number Com 35597: 

 To Whom It May Concern: 

 I wanted to follow up on the following Pennsylvania Right to Know Act request, copied below, and originally submitted on Dec. 15, 2025. Please let me know when I can expect to receive a response. You had assigned it reference number #Com 35597. 

 Thanks for your help, and let me know if further clarification is needed. 

 View request history, upload responsive documents, and report problems here: https://www.muckrock.com/respond/2223476/

 

 Filed via MuckRock.com E-mail (Preferred): 199615-39590937@requests.muckrock.com 

 PLEASE NOTE OUR NEW ADDRESS For mailed responses, please address (see note): MuckRock News DEPT MR 199615 263 Huntington Ave Boston, MA 02115 

 PLEASE NOTE: This request is not filed by a MuckRock staff member, but is being sent through MuckRock by the above in order to better track, share, and manage public records requests. Also note that improperly addressed (i.e., with the requester's name rather than "MuckRock News" and the department number) requests might be returned as undeliverable.

communications.subject: Iowa Open Records Law Request: Open Records Request: Whooster
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Iowa Open Records Law, I hereby request the following records:

- All contracts, licenses, MOUs, MOAs, data agreements, letters of intent, State of Work documents, Scope of Work documents, and other agreement documents with Whooster that were active any time between 1/1/2023 and 11/14/2025.
- All financial documents or records of financial transactions, exchanges or obligations related to Whooster. Responsive materials include all invoices, bills, receipts, check stubs, or any other records, including those possessed in an electronic format.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

e

communications.communication:
Greetings,

My name is Adam Bowersox and I'm with the City of Altoona. We received a FOIA request from your group and we are having trouble submitting the files in the way that you had requested. I've attached the original request here. Your system isn't allowing us to send the file as it is asking for an email that is associated with that request. We have the files ready to go, if you could reach out to myself and Lieutenant Harris and advise, we'll be happy to send the files.

Thank you!

Adam



Adam Bowersox

City of Altoona, 900 Venbury Drive
Altoona, IA 50009

Cell: 515-975-2796

title: Drone as First Responder program contract and details

communications.subject: Nevada Public Records Act Request: Drone as First Responder program contract and details
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Nevada Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

1. A copy of all Requests for Proposal (or equivalent solicitations) and vendor bids and supplemental materials associated with this agency’s procurement of devices and services related to the establishment and execution of a Drone-as-First-Responder (DFR) program

2. A copy of this agency’s  contract (as well as all amendments, attachments, and exhibits) for devices and services used in its DFR program

3. Copies of all policies, directives, guidelines, and procedures governing this agency’s use of drones and the execution of operations under its DFR program, including documentation describing the device models and technical specifications of the devices used and any policies regarding the “curtilage” of a person’s property

4. A copy of all data sharing agreements and lists of other entities with which this agency shares or receives information. This includes the list of all agencies that receive notifications based on this agency’s list of license plates and vehicles of interest (often referred to as a “hot list”) and the list of all agencies that have access to all vehicle scans conducted by this agency’s devices

5. A copy of all manuals and operational references for this agency’s use of drones and the implementation and use of the DFR program. This includes all policies, procedures, and manuals related to the following elements of the execution of the drone and DFR program: 
a. appropriate deployment and use of the drone or drones; 
b. collection of camera footage, audio, and other data;
c. retention of footage, audio, and other data;
d. sharing of footage, audio, and other data, including via third-party networks and across jurisdictions; 
e. appropriate positioning of drone cameras and other payloads during flights;
f. the appropriate protection of collected, stored, and shared audio, footage, and other data
g. the policies and mechanisms used for auditing drone and DFR use, including appropriate investigatory use and access
h. the policies and mechanisms of consequences of unauthorized and inappropriate access to the drone or DFR systems

6. All materials sufficient to show the complete list of features and integration capabilities associated with the drone or DFR programs, including all materials related to device or DFR program integration with automated license plate readers (ALPRs), gunshot detection, video analytics, AI-enabled analysis, and other datasets, surveillance tools, and analytical systems.

7. All communications between a representative of this agency and a representative of the private entity from which this agency has procured DFR program devices and services for the period from January 1, 2025 through the current date.

8. A copy of the drone flight log for the period from January 1, 2025, through September 22, 2025 including the date and time of the flight, the investigatory purpose of the flight, the drone operator, and any other segregable information

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Vivien Fleck
Please submit your request via our online portal through Sparkspolice.com. We cannot complete your request through fax.

Pursuant to the Nevada Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

1. A copy of all Requests for Proposal (or equivalent solicitations) and vendor bids and supplemental materials associated with this agency’s procurement of devices and services related to the establishment and execution of a Drone-as-First-Responder (DFR) program

2. A copy of this agency’s contract (as well as all amendments, attachments, and exhibits) for devices and services used in its DFR program

3. Copies of all policies, directives, guidelines, and procedures governing this agency’s use of drones and the execution of operations under its DFR program, including documentation describing the device models and technical specifications of the devices used and any policies regarding the “curtilage” of a person’s property

4. A copy of all data sharing agreements and lists of other entities with which this agency shares or receives information. This includes the list of all agencies that receive notifications based on this agency’s list of license plates and vehicles of interest (often referred to as a “hot list”) and the list of all agencies that have access to all vehicle scans conducted by this agency’s devices

5. A copy of all manuals and operational references for this agency’s use of drones and the implementation and use of the DFR program. This includes all policies, procedures, and manuals related to the following elements of the execution of the drone and DFR program:
a. appropriate deployment and use of the drone or drones;
b. collection of camera footage, audio, and other data;
c. retention of footage, audio, and other data;
d. sharing of footage, audio, and other data, including via third-party networks and across jurisdictions;
e. appropriate positioning of drone cameras and other payloads during flights;
f. the appropriate protection of collected, stored, and shared audio, footage, and other data
g. the policies and mechanisms used for auditing drone and DFR use, including appropriate investigatory use and access
h. the policies and mechanisms of consequences of unauthorized and inappropriate access to the drone or DFR systems

6. All materials sufficient to show the complete list of features and integration capabilities associated with the drone or DFR programs, including all materials related to device or DFR program integration with automated license plate readers (ALPRs), gunshot detection, video analytics, AI-enabled analysis, and other datasets, surveillance tools, and analytical systems.

7. All communications between a representative of this agency and a representative of the private entity from which this agency has procured DFR program devices and services for the period from January 1, 2025 through the current date.

8. A copy of the drone flight log for the period from January 1, 2025, through September 22, 2025 including the date and time of the flight, the investigatory purpose of the flight, the drone operator, and any other segregable information

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Vivien Fleck
